raw_output: The first human mission to land on the Moon was successfully accomplished by astronauts Neil Armstrong and Edwin "Buzz" Aldrin of the Apollo 11 mission. Armstrong became the first person to step onto the lunar surface on July 20, while Aldrin remained in the Lunar Module Eagle during the descent. Mission commander for Apollo 11 was Michael Collins who piloted the Command Module Columbia and orbited the Moon while Armstrong and Aldrin descended to the lunar surface. This significant event occurred during the Apollo program, a space exploration initiative led by NASA, the United States' space agency.
